content.text: Cost Analysis

Azure Cost Analysis is the primary tool for understanding where cloud spend is going. It queries the Cost Management data model — which aggregates metered usage, reservations, marketplace charges, and credits — and returns cost breakdowns by any combination of scope, time period, service, resource group, tag, or billing dimension. The same underlying API powers both the Azure Portal Cost Analysis blade and programmatic reporting pipelines.

attrs.alt: Cost analysis query anatomy: scope, type, time frame, aggregation, groupBy, and result rows

content.text: Cost Analysis Scopes

A scope determines which resources and charges are included in the query. Scopes form a hierarchy: querying at a higher scope includes all child scopes. The same API endpoint accepts any scope via the ARM path.
Scope
ARM Path
What It Includes
Subscription
/subscriptions/{id}
All resource groups and resources under the subscription
Resource Group
/subscriptions/{id}/resourceGroups/{rg}
Resources in that resource group only
Management Group
/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/{mg}
All subscriptions under the management group
Billing Account (MCA)
/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{id}
All billing profiles and their subscriptions
Billing Profile (MCA)
/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{id}/billingProfiles/{id}
All subscriptions in that billing profile
Invoice Section
.../invoiceSections/{id}
Subscriptions linked to this invoice section
EA Enrollment
/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{enrollment}
All departments and subscriptions in the EA
Running Cost Queries via Azure CLI

# Month-to-date cost by service for a subscription
az costmanagement query \
  --scope "/subscriptions/<subscription-id>" \
  --type ActualCost \
  --timeframe MonthToDate \
  --dataset-aggregation '{"totalCost":{"name":"Cost","function":"Sum"}}' \
  --dataset-grouping '[{"type":"Dimension","name":"ServiceName"}]' \
  --output table

# Last month cost by resource group
az costmanagement query \
  --scope "/subscriptions/<subscription-id>" \
  --type ActualCost \
  --timeframe TheLastMonth \
  --dataset-aggregation '{"totalCost":{"name":"Cost","function":"Sum"}}' \
  --dataset-grouping '[{"type":"Dimension","name":"ResourceGroup"}]' \
  --query "properties.rows" \
  --output json
Cost Queries via the REST API
Basic Cost by Service
Daily Cost Trend with Granularity
Filtering Cost Queries
Filters restrict the result set to specific services, resource groups, or tags. The filter syntax uses OData-style operators: `eq` (equals), `in` (in list), `and` for compound conditions.
Filter by Tag
Filter by Multiple Services (compound AND)
ActualCost vs AmortizedCost

The `type` field of a cost query controls how reservation and savings plan charges appear. `ActualCost` shows charges as they hit the invoice — reservation purchases appear as large one-time charges in the month purchased. `AmortizedCost` distributes reservation costs evenly across the days in the reservation term, making daily trends smoother and more representative of true ongoing cost.
Query Type
Reservation Handling
Best For
ActualCost
Reservation purchase = full upfront cost in purchase month; utilization period = $0
Invoice reconciliation, accounts payable
AmortizedCost
Reservation cost spread evenly per day over term; each day shows fractional cost
Cost trends, showback reports, capacity planning
Usage
Only metered usage charges; no reservation, credits, or adjustments
Meter-level analysis; debugging specific service consumption

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

scope = "/subscriptions/<subscription-id>"
url = f"https://management.azure.com{scope}/providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/query?api-version=2023-11-01"

def query_cost(cost_type: str, timeframe: str = "MonthToDate") -> float:
    """Return total cost using the specified type."""
    q = {
        "type": cost_type,
        "timeframe": timeframe,
        "dataSet": {
            "granularity": "None",
            "aggregation": {"totalCost": {"name": "Cost", "function": "Sum"}}
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=q)
    rows = resp.json()["properties"]["rows"]
    return float(rows[0][0]) if rows else 0.0

actual    = query_cost("ActualCost")
amortized = query_cost("AmortizedCost")
print(f"ActualCost:    {actual:>12,.2f}")
print(f"AmortizedCost: {amortized:>12,.2f}")
print(f"Difference:    {actual - amortized:>+12,.2f}  (reservation lump-sum effect)")
Management Group Scope: Cross-Subscription Cost Queries

Management groups aggregate subscriptions into a hierarchy. Querying at the management group scope returns costs for all subscriptions in that group and all child groups, making it the right scope for organization-wide showback and chargeback automation.

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

# Query at management group scope
mg_name = "contoso-root"
scope = f"/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/{mg_name}"
url = f"https://management.azure.com{scope}/providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/query?api-version=2023-11-01"

query = {
    "type": "ActualCost",
    "timeframe": "TheLastMonth",
    "dataSet": {
        "granularity": "None",
        "aggregation": {
            "totalCost": {"name": "Cost", "function": "Sum"}
        },
        "grouping": [
            {"type": "Dimension", "name": "SubscriptionName"},
            {"type": "Dimension", "name": "ServiceName"}
        ],
        "sorting": [{"name": "Cost", "direction": "Descending"}]
    }
}

resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=query)
data = resp.json()

columns = [c["name"] for c in data["properties"]["columns"]]
rows = data["properties"]["rows"]

# Aggregate by subscription
from collections import defaultdict
sub_costs: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
for row in rows:
    d = dict(zip(columns, row))
    sub_costs[d.get("SubscriptionName", "Unknown")] += d.get("Cost", 0)

print(f"Cross-subscription cost breakdown (last month)")
print(f"{'Subscription':<40} {'Total Cost':>15}")
print("-" * 58)
for sub, cost in sorted(sub_costs.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True):
    print(f"{sub:<40} {cost:>15,.2f}")

grand_total = sum(sub_costs.values())
print("-" * 58)
print(f"{'Grand Total':<40} {grand_total:>15,.2f}")
Scheduled Cost Reports: GitHub Actions Pipeline
Cost reporting pipelines run on a schedule to deliver weekly or monthly cost summaries. The following GitHub Actions workflow queries the Cost Management API and posts a summary to Slack.

name: Weekly Azure Cost Report

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 8 * * MON"   # Every Monday 08:00 UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  id-token: write    # for OIDC federation
  contents: read

jobs:
  cost-report:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Azure Login (OIDC)
        uses: azure/login@v2
        with:
          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pip install azure-identity requests

      - name: Generate Cost Report
        env:
          SUBSCRIPTION_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}
          SLACK_WEBHOOK:   ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: python scripts/weekly_cost_report.py

# scripts/weekly_cost_report.py
import os
import requests
from collections import defaultdict
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

sub_id = os.environ["SUBSCRIPTION_ID"]
scope = f"/subscriptions/{sub_id}"
url = f"https://management.azure.com{scope}/providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/query?api-version=2023-11-01"

query = {
    "type": "ActualCost",
    "timeframe": "TheLastWeek",
    "dataSet": {
        "granularity": "None",
        "aggregation": {"totalCost": {"name": "Cost", "function": "Sum"}},
        "grouping": [{"type": "Dimension", "name": "ServiceName"}],
        "sorting": [{"name": "Cost", "direction": "Descending"}]
    }
}

resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=query)
data = resp.json()

columns = [c["name"] for c in data["properties"]["columns"]]
rows = data["properties"]["rows"]
currency = data["properties"].get("currency", "USD")

total = sum(r[columns.index("Cost")] for r in rows)
top5 = rows[:5]

lines = [f"*Azure Weekly Cost Summary* — Last 7 days\n"]
lines.append(f"*Total: {total:,.2f} {currency}*\n")
lines.append("Top services:")
for row in top5:
    d = dict(zip(columns, row))
    lines.append(f"  • {d['ServiceName']}: {d['Cost']:,.2f} {currency}")

payload = {"text": "\n".join(lines)}
webhook = os.environ.get("SLACK_WEBHOOK")
if webhook:
    slack_resp = requests.post(webhook, json=payload)
    print(f"Slack response: {slack_resp.status_code}")
else:
    print("\n".join(lines))
Saving and Exporting Cost Views

Cost Management views are reusable query configurations saved to a billing scope. The API supports creating, listing, and deleting views, allowing teams to persist their standard cost breakdowns without re-specifying query parameters each time.
Common Cost Analysis Patterns and Gotchas
Pattern / Gotcha
Detail
Query returns empty rows for new subscriptions
New subscriptions have no cost data; the API returns an empty rows array rather than an error. Check the subscription creation date before querying.
Management group queries require extra permission
Billing Reader at the management group scope OR Cost Management Reader — not just Contributor on child subscriptions.
Max 1000 rows per page
Use nextLink for pagination; large groupings (e.g., groupBy ResourceId) can easily exceed this.
Daily granularity UsageDate format
The date column returns an integer like 20240115 (YYYYMMDD), not an ISO string. Parse with strptime('%Y%m%d').
AmortizedCost not available for all scopes
AmortizedCost requires a billing scope (MCA or EA) or a subscription; it is not available at the resource group scope.
Timeframe 'TheLastWeek' is 7 trailing days
'TheLastWeek' is not calendar week (Mon–Sun); it is the 7 days before today. Use Custom with explicit from/to for calendar weeks.
Summary

Azure Cost Analysis queries the Cost Management API using a combination of scope, time frame, cost type (ActualCost vs AmortizedCost), and grouping/filtering dimensions. The query API is the programmatic equivalent of the portal's Cost Analysis blade and supports any level of the billing or resource hierarchy as a scope. ActualCost aligns with invoices; AmortizedCost is better for trend analysis and showback. Always sort by Cost descending and paginate via nextLink for large result sets.
Scope determines which resources are included: subscription, resource group, management group, or billing scope
ActualCost matches invoice totals; AmortizedCost spreads reservation costs daily for smoother trends
GroupBy supports Dimension (ServiceName, ResourceGroup, etc.) and Tag (custom tag key-value)
Filter combines AND/OR conditions on dimensions and tags — use 'In' for multi-value matching
The query API returns up to 1000 rows per page; follow nextLink for complete result sets
